concerns.text: Task T013 implements a 'Linear Model' for data generation, explicitly noting a deviation from Spec FR-001 which mandates a 'triangular kernel model'. While the plan notes this ambiguity, the task silently relaxes the FR-001 constraint without a formal spec amendment or explicit 'staged' flag in the task description, violating the Single Source of Truth principle.

Task T023 implements the bandwidth fallback using the literal string '[deferred]' as a value ('fallback to [deferred] * range'). This violates FR-003's requirement for a 'mandatory fallback to a fixed bandwidth of [deferred] of the running variable range'. The task fails to resolve the deferred value into a concrete implementation parameter or a configurable default, rendering the FR-003 constraint untestable and the code non-deterministic.

Task T034 instructs the Monte-Carlo loop to iterate '[deferred] times'. This directly violates FR-006 which mandates 'a minimum of 1,000 Monte-Carlo replications'. The task silently relaxes the FR-006 constraint by leaving the iteration count undefined, preventing verification of the 6-hour runtime constraint (SC-004) and the stability of metric estimation.

Task T032 sets the nominal confidence level to 'default set to a standard nominal level per plan' while the plan defaults to '[deferred]'. This violates SC-001 which requires measuring coverage against a 'nominal [deferred] level'. The task fails to define the target value (e.g., 0.95) required to calculate 'Coverage', making the acceptance criterion in US-3 (Scenario 1) untestable.

Task T018 defines the MCAR failure condition as 'p < 0.05' but the Spec US-1 Acceptance Scenario 1 requires 'p > 0.05' for independence. While the logic in T018 is statistically correct for a *failure* (dependence), the task description conflates the success/failure criteria without explicitly mapping to the Spec's 'Given/When/Then' structure, creating a risk of silent relaxation of the verification logic.
